question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getHelmReleaseReadyStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user inject an empty conditions list into the HelmRelease status, causing conditions.stream().filter(...).findFirst() to return Optional.empty(), which maps to orElse(DOWN), resulting in a DOWN health status that is cached and served to Kubernetes liveness probes, causing >=30% of monitor nodes to be restarted?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: mapStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that mapStatus() returns the static shared Mono<Health> DOWN instance, which is a hot Mono that could be subscribed to by multiple concurrent health checks simultaneously, causing a thundering herd of DOWN responses that triggers Kubernetes to restart >=30% of nodes at the same time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: registerGauge()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @PostConstruct registerGauge() method's registration of a gauge tied to the static RELEASE_UP AtomicInteger to cause a metric scraping system (e.g., Prometheus) to observe a sustained 0 value and trigger an automated remediation action that shuts down >=30% of network processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getHelmRelease()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Kubernetes RBAC permissions granted to the monitor service account — which must be able to read pods and HelmRelease resources — to enumerate pod names and HelmRelease names, then craft a targeted attack that causes the health indicator to resolve to DOWN for >=30% of monitor instances?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: createHelmReleaseHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the cache(properties.getCacheExpiry(), ...) time-based cache expiry to cause a denial-of-service window where, during the cache refresh period, a slow or failing Kubernetes API causes all health checks to return UNKNOWN, and if this coincides with Kubernetes liveness probe checks, >=30% of nodes are restarted?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that health() does not implement any rate limiting or circuit breaking, so that a flood of health check requests during a cache miss window causes multiple concurrent Kubernetes API calls, overwhelming the API server and causing all calls to fail, resulting in UNKNOWN health for all monitor instances and restarting >=30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getHelmReleaseReadyStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RESOURCE_DEFINITION_CONTEXT (group=helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io, kind=HelmRelease, version=v2) by causing the Kubernetes API server to return a 404 for the HelmRelease resource, which causes resource to be null, triggering a NullPointerException on resource.getAdditionalProperties(), swallowed by onErrorComplete(), resulting in UNKNOWN health and restarting >=30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: mapStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DEPENDENCY_NOT_READY constant comparison in mapStatus() to inject a HelmRelease condition with reason='DependencyNotReady' across multiple releases simultaneously, causing all monitor instances to report UNKNOWN health, which when interpreted by an orchestration system as unhealthy, triggers shutdown of >=30% of network processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: createHelmReleaseHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Mono.fromCallable(this::getHelmRelease) blocking call executed on a reactive thread to cause thread starvation in the reactive scheduler, preventing other health checks from completing and causing a cascading failure where >=30% of monitor nodes appear unhealthy to Kubernetes liveness probes?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getHelmRelease()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the synchronous blocking call client.pods().withName(hostname).get() inside a Mono.fromCallable() — which runs on a reactive thread — to cause a thread block when the Kubernetes API is slow, starving the reactive scheduler and causing health() to never complete, leading Kubernetes liveness probes to time out and restart >=30% of nodes?
